title: Psychotria neogarretii
uses: Psychotria neogarretii is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines and Indonesia. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The plant is also believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, it is used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Psychotria neogarretii.
taxonomy: Psychotria neogarretii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Psychotria, which comprises about 1,800 species of tropical plants. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with approximately 13,000 species. This family includes plants commonly known as coffee, gardenias, and periwinkles, among others. Psychotria neogarretii, like other members of the Rubiaceae family, is native to tropical regions around the world.
